headlines.title: JD Vance holds peace talks with Iran as Trump threatens strikes over Hormuz
headlines.summary: The president's warning underscores persistent tensions even as Vance said in Switzerland that he hoped the two sides would “turn over a new leaf” in their relationship.

headlines.title: For cash-strapped farmers, deal to end Iran fighting comes too late
headlines.summary: Some of the president’s strongest supporters are hurting as midterm elections approach.

headlines.title: Chaotic run-up to U.S.-Iran talks signals steep hurdles ahead
headlines.summary: Neither Washington nor Tehran wants the peace process to collapse completely, but diplomats say they expect a process full of challenges and distrust.

headlines.title: Trump said Italy’s prime minister ‘begged’ for a photo. She says that’s false.
headlines.summary: Italy’s right-wing prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, was labeled Europe’s “Trump whisperer,” but the war in Iran turned their friendship into a liability for her.

headlines.title: War-weary Iranians see glimmers of relief as Trump deal takes shape
headlines.summary: As details emerged of an initial agreement between Washington and Tehran, Iranians voiced muted hopes for peace and prosperity.

headlines.title: An Ebola closure cuts off a lifeline between DRC’s Goma and Rwanda
headlines.summary: Health officials defend the restrictions, but traders say the measures have disrupted the flow of goods and income.

headlines.title: US, Iran agree roadmap for final deal as mediators hail ‘progress’ in talks
headlines.summary: US, Iran establish committee to oversee talks and a 'de-confliction cell' to end attacks in Lebanon, mediators say.

headlines.title: Mourners gather to remember Lebanese conservationist killed by Israel
headlines.summary: People gathered in Beirut in memory of beloved turtle conservationist Mona Khalil, killed in Israeli attack.

headlines.title: Ramiro Valdes, one of Cuba’s last living revolutionary figures, dies at 94
headlines.summary: Valdes was close ally of Castro brothers and helped found Cuba's notorious intelligence apparatus.

headlines.title: Ethiopian prime minister’s party easily wins parliamentary election
headlines.summary: Nobel Peace Prize winner PM Abiy Ahmed will stay in power, as analysts warn of renewed conflicts in Ethiopia.

headlines.title: Colombia Election: Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella wins, initial count shows
headlines.summary: Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.

headlines.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after Israeli airstrike on her home
headlines.summary: Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.

headlines.title: Trump threatens to 'hit Iran very hard again' while Vance is in Switzerland for talks
headlines.summary: President Trump has threatened further attacks on Iran while Vice President Vance attended talks with Iranian officials in Switzerland on Sunday.

headlines.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
headlines.summary: Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.

headlines.title: Trump claims vandals damaged D.C. Reflecting Pool, and says it will be drained again
headlines.summary: The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.

headlines.title: Intra-Asian travel viewed as opportunity amid tensions
headlines.summary: Turmoil in the Middle East has reshaped the global travel landscape. Long-haul travel is among the hardest hit, while intra-regional travel could become a bright spot for many tourism-dependent Asian economies, according to industry analysts.

headlines.title: Report: UK PM Starmer could resign
headlines.summary: LONDON — United Kingdom's Prime Minister Keir Starmer was expected to resign on Monday and set out a timetable for his departure, according to The Observer, a local newspaper.

headlines.title: Nepal looks to strengthen collaboration with China
headlines.summary: Nepal's new government is looking to strengthen political engagement with China and expand cooperation in areas including connectivity, technology, governance and people-to-people exchanges, Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal says.

headlines.title: Mediators unveil steps on further US-Iran engagements toward final deal
headlines.summary: BURGENSTOCK, Switzerland - The first session of US-Iran talks was conducted in a positive and constructive atmosphere, with encouraging progress made, including the creation of a mechanism for further technical talks, according to a joint statement issued by Qatar and Pakistan on Monday.

headlines.title: US-Japan military drills raise concern
headlines.summary: Japan and the United States have launched a large-scale joint military exercise, raising concerns of destabilizing regional security and further heightening tensions between Japan and its neighboring countries.

headlines.title: Yoga has become world’s largest community celebration, says PM Modi
headlines.summary: PM Modi celebrates International Yoga Day in Kolkata, emphasising yoga's role in healthy ageing and global community connection.

headlines.title: U.P. Muslim MPs urge government to refrain from targeted action on heritage structures
headlines.summary: U.P. Muslim MPs urge government to protect heritage sites, criticizing targeted demolitions and external interference in India's internal matters.

headlines.title: ‘Operation Tiger’ successful, declares Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde
headlines.summary: Maharashtra's Eknath Shinde declares 'Operation Tiger' successful as six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs prepare to merge with his faction.

headlines.title: From Shillong to seas: Armed forces celebrate International Day of Yoga across India
headlines.summary: Armed forces across India, led by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, celebrated International Day of Yoga, promoting fitness and resilience.

headlines.title: Over 20 lakh medical aspirants appear for NEET-UG 2026 re-exam amidst heightened security
headlines.summary: Candidates arrive at NEET-UG re-test centres across India, facing tight security amid preparations for a fair examination.

headlines.title: US and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say
headlines.summary: Reuters: US and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say.

headlines.title: Fifty-four injured and 18 missing after explosion at Qatar LNG site, authorities say
headlines.summary: Reuters: Fifty-four injured and 18 missing after explosion at Qatar LNG site, authorities say.

headlines.title: People caught vandalizing Washington Reflecting Pool to be fully prosecuted, US Attorney Pirro says
headlines.summary: Reuters: People caught vandalizing Washington Reflecting Pool to be fully prosecuted, US Attorney Pirro says.

headlines.title: Ramiro Valdes, lauded as hero of Cuban revolution, dies at 94
headlines.summary: Reuters: Ramiro Valdes, lauded as hero of Cuban revolution, dies at 94.

headlines.title: Shipping slows after Iran says it has again shut the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Reuters: Shipping slows after Iran says it has again shut the Strait of Hormuz.

hackerNews.title: Apertus – Open Foundation Model for Sovereign AI
hackerNews.summary: Developed by the Swiss AI Initiative as a collaborative effort between EPFL, ETH Zurich, and CSCS. Open weights, open data, open science.

hackerNews.title: Did my old job only exist because of fraud?
hackerNews.summary: Early in my software engineering career, the UK-based startup I worked at, GenieDB, was taken over by a US Venture Capital fund, Frost VP, owned by Stuart Frost. I was functionally the only piece that came to the US. The code was rebuilt, the rest of the team eventually rotated out, even the core strategy was replaced. But I was early in my career and excited to be working in the VC tech-startup world. I ended up making my life in the US, so this was a pretty pivotal phase in my life.

hackerNews.summary: One model to command them all

hackerNews.title: Everything is logarithms
hackerNews.summary: Hey, it looks like you have Javascript disabled. There's math on this page that's supposed to be rendered by Javascript, so it might not make sense!

hackerNews.title: There is minimal downside to switching to open models
hackerNews.summary: There was a time not too long ago when using Linux entailed some professional risk 1 . First there was compatibility: you may not have been able to render a Word document or PowerPoint correctly, and you might have had to trust Open Office’s export capability to render docs the way you wanted. There might have been specialty file formats you couldn’t easily view and so couldn’t collaborate. And second, the software ecosystem was just worse generally. There were lots of half-build open-source projects trying to achieve the functionality of mainstream software, but they always had rough edges. I, embarrassingly, stayed on Windows until I left academia over Matlab.

hackerNews.title: 1983 Northern Telecom Commodore Phone
hackerNews.summary: 1983 Northern Telecom Commodore Phone. The Curious Case of the 1983 Commodore Phone: A Canadian-Only Tech Oddity.

hackerNews.title: Good results fine tuning a local LLM like Qwen 3:0.6B to categorize questions
hackerNews.summary: As a fun personal project, I have been working on a chatbot for answering general questions about my household on anything from maintenance questions to doctor’s appointments. The general idea is that the chatbot will get its household knowledge through RAG from querying a vector database, but for better results I have made the vector searches metadata aware. Basically, I am running questions through a pre-processing step to categorize questions into known metadata categories (e.g. pool, car, hvac, cooking). The main goal of this is to narrow down the search space for vector ranking to only indexed entries that match the category of the question. As an example, the question “When did we replace our pool pump?” will be mapped to a category called “pool” before querying the Index database.

hackerNews.summary: PowerFox is a secure, modern, and open-source web browser for Mac OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard, 10.5 Leopard, and 10.4 Tiger for Intel and PowerPC Macs.

hackerNews.summary: Being responsible with powerful technology starts with knowing who is using it. Identity verification helps us prevent abuse, enforce our usage policies, and comply with legal obligations.
